_comment: Result-count regression baseline for the sub-word frequency guard (issue #156). Each demo entry pins a broad multi-word query, its multi-word expansion terms, and the real corpus frequency (document count) of every constituent sub-word. tests/js/result-count-baseline.test.js drives the real scolta.js guard against a synthetic pagefind corpus built from these counts and asserts the merged result count stays within [band.min, band.max]. Frequencies are from real pagefind measurements: recipes/terra-collecta from frequency-guard-sweep-verification.md; git-manual from a live per-language (English partition) measurement on gitmastery.ddev.site (2026-05-31). The guard's denominator is the language-scoped corpus, so git-manual uses the 286-doc English partition. The band catches BOTH a recall collapse (sub-word block removed, as in commit 690a2288: count drops below min) AND a precision spike (high-frequency noise admitted: count exceeds max). referenceLiveCounts records the union counts measured live for traceability; the deterministic CI assertion uses the synthetic union model (no MAX_PAGEFIND_RESULTS-capped overlap) and its own band. social-network and wikipedia are exercised by the live demo regression (tests/demo-regression.sh), not here.

git-manual._comment: English partition (286 docs). At 5% nearly every candidate sub-word exceeds the guard (only rare terms like 'shelve' pass), so recovery is minimal — matching the measured +1/query for the English partition. expectsRecovery is false: the band guards the PRECISION ceiling (count must not spike toward the all-sub-words count of ~183 if the threshold were wrongly raised), not recall.
